calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-28-2022 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-24-2022 Continued To Apr-20-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing [Concurrently Filed With Request For Entry Of Default Judgment, Declaration Of Carl Jean-Felix]
Off Calendar Mar-03-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Prove-Up Hearing as to defaulted defendant CTC TRIAD, Inc., a corporation, dba Silver Lining Transport.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:17 a.m.

Kathleen A. Sauer, Esq. and Michael Marcelli, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Redwood Fire and Casualty Insurance Company, a Nebraska corporation.
Carl Jean-Felix, Field Audit Supervisor and Custodian of Records for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Ms. Sauer, along with Messrs. Jean-Felix and Marcelli, appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant CTC TRIAD, Inc., a corporation, dba Silver Lining Transport.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract was filed on 02/08/2021.
Substitute service of plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendant CTC TRIAD, Inc., a corporation, dba Silver Lining Transport, occurred on 02/25/2021, and was effectuated on 03/07/2021.
Default as to defendant CTC TRIAD, Inc., a corporation, dba Silver Lining Transport, was entered on April 16, 2021.
DOE defendants were dismissed on 09/27/2021.

Plaintiff Redwood Fire and Casualty Insurance Company, a Nebraska corporation, submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:

Exhibit 1: Redwood Fire and Casualty Insurance Policy dated 07/12/2018................ID/EVID
Exhibit 2: Redwood Fire and Casualty Insurance Policy dated 07/03/2019................ID/EVID
Exhibit 3: Workers Compensation Insurance Premium dated 11/16/2020....................ID/EVID
Exhibit 4: Workers Compensation Insurance Premium dated 11/10/2020....................ID/EVID
Exhibit 6: Workers Compensation Insurance Premium dated 08/30/2021....................ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Redwood Fire and Casualty Insurance Company, a Nebraska corporation, and against the defaulted defendant CTC TRIAD, Inc., a corporation, dba Silver Lining Transport, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $35,699.94.
Prejudgment Interest - $4,447.49.
Attorney Fees - $2,825.00.
Costs - $691.00.

Total - $43,663.43.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Plaintiff STIPULATES to the return of the exhibits.

Proceedings concluded at 10:37 a.m.
